title: Loxogramme vittariiformis
intro: Loxogramme vittariiformis is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Loxogramme vittariiformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Loxogramme vittariiformis is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tonics, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Loxogramme vittariiformis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries such as China, India, and Southeast Asia. Loxogramme vittariiformis belongs to the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, it is a part of the larger group of polypody ferns. The species Loxogramme vittariiformis is often found growing in shaded, humid environments.
